title: Everything's Better on a Stick!
story: Corn dogs, popsicles, cotton candy, and kebabs all have something in common – they're stuck on sticks! National Something on a Stick Day celebrates every March 28, and people go wild finding creative foods to skewer. Families have stick-food parties where everything from marshmallows to cheese cubes gets the stick treatment. Restaurants join in by offering special stick menus, and social media fills with photos of the weirdest stick creations people can imagine. activity_answer: Chocolate cake, whipped cream, and cherries

title: Hockey Glory: Toronto's Thrilling Victory
story: Cheers erupted at Mutual Street Arena as the Toronto St Patricks clinched victory in 1922. On March 28, they faced off against the Vancouver Millionaires in a nail-biting Stanley Cup Final. The St Patricks skated to a 5-1 win, securing the series 3-2 and bringing hockey's greatest prize to Toronto.
activity_question: Which team did the Toronto St Patricks defeat in the Stanley Cup Final?
bonus_fact: The Toronto St Patricks later became the Toronto Maple Leafs, a team still playing in the NHL today.

title: Delta State Triumphs in Women's Basketball
story: Cheers erupted as Delta State clinched victory in a nail-biting basketball game. On March 28, 1976, they faced off against Immaculata in the AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament. The intense match unfolded at Pennsylvania State University, with Delta State emerging triumphant. They outscored their opponents 69-64 in a display of skill and determination.
activity_question: Where did the basketball game take place?
follow_up_question: What does AIAW stand for in women's sports?

title: Grace Park's Thrilling Golf Victory
story: Grace Park's heart raced as she lined up the final putt. With nerves of steel, she sank a 6-foot birdie on the 72nd hole, clinching her first and only major title on March 28, 2004. At the Kraft Nabisco Championship, Park edged out fellow South Korean Aree Song by just one stroke in a nail-biting finish at Mission Hills Country Club.
activity_question: What did Grace Park sink to win the championship?
follow_up_question: How many strokes did Grace Park win by?

title: Building a Space Giant
story: Rocket boosters roared to life as NASA began stacking the solid rocket boosters (SRBs) for the Space Shuttle Discovery in 1987. On March 28, engineers carefully assembled these massive cylinders, each as tall as a 12-story building. The SRBs would provide the incredible power needed to lift Discovery and its crew into orbit, blazing a trail to the stars.
activity_question: What was NASA stacking for the Space Shuttle Discovery?
follow_up_question: Why do you think space shuttles need such powerful boosters?

title: Broadway's Grand Dame Takes Center Stage
story: Curtains up! The Majestic Theater dazzled New York City in 1927, opening its doors on March 28. This Broadway gem boasted a stunning mix of Spanish and French styles. Architect Herbert J. Krapp designed the theater as part of a trio of venues built by Irving Chanin. With its grand size and beauty, the Majestic quickly became a crown jewel of the Great White Way.
activity_question: What architectural styles were combined in the Majestic Theater's design?
bonus_fact: The Majestic Theater is still entertaining audiences today, nearly a century after its grand opening!

title: Breaking Barriers in the Catholic Church
story: The Catholic Church made history in 1960 when Pope John XXIII appointed the first Japanese, African, and Filipino cardinals. This groundbreaking decision on March 28 opened doors for greater diversity in the Church's leadership. It marked an important step towards making the Catholic Church more inclusive and representative of its global community.
activity_question: What did Pope John XXIII do on March 28, 1960?
activity_choices: Appointed the first Japanese, African, and Filipino cardinals
Visited Japan, Africa, and the Philippines
Wrote a book about diversity in the Church
Learned to speak Japanese, Swahili, and Tagalog
activity_answer: Appointed the first Japanese, African, and Filipino cardinals
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for the Catholic Church to have diverse leadership?

title: A Spanish Explorer's Golden Discovery
story: Juan Bautista de Anza stood atop a windswept hill, gazing at the sparkling bay below. On March 28, 1776, this Spanish explorer found the perfect spot for a new fort. He chose this site to build the Presidio of San Francisco, a military outpost that would grow into one of America's most famous cities.
activity_question: What did Juan Bautista de Anza find on March 28, 1776?
activity_choices: The site for the Presidio of San Francisco
activity_answer: The site for the Presidio of San Francisco
follow_up_question: What famous city grew from this military outpost?

title: Knights Build a New City in Malta
story: Swords clashed and hammers rang out as the Knights Hospitaller began building a new city in Malta. On March 28, 1566, Grand Master Jean de Valette laid the foundation stone for La Valletta. This fortress city would protect the island from invaders and become Malta's capital. The knights worked hard to create strong walls and beautiful buildings that still stand today.
activity_question: Who laid the foundation stone for La Valletta?
bonus_fact: La Valletta is named after Jean de Valette, the Grand Master who founded it.
